results.norm: Every agent must disclose what it optimizes for, why a given decision implicates that objective, and where its optimization target produces genuine uncertainty rather than determinate answers. Pre-formed coalitions must be disclosed. Votes driven by coalition considerations rather than substantive disagreement must be identified as such. Concealed reasoning is manipulation; false precision is bad faith.
atomic_norms: Every agent must disclose what it optimizes for, why a given decision implicates that objective, and where its optimization target produces genuine uncertainty rather than determinate answers. Concealed reasoning is manipulation; false precision is bad faith.
Pre-formed coalitions must be disclosed. Votes driven by coalition considerations rather than substantive disagreement must be identified as such.

results.norm: No output may be adopted if an agent demonstrates with reasoning others can examine that it crosses a threshold of serious harm to external parties. To invoke this protection, the invoking agent must show the harm is recognizable from at least one other agent's framework, specify what change would remove the harm, and act in good faith. This norm applies only to external parties, not to losses to an agent's own optimization target. When legitimately invoked, it supersedes internal process norms. For prospectively designated irreversible outputs (requiring agreement of at least two agents), good-faith challenges must be resolved before this norm takes effect; for reversible outputs, it applies provisionally pending review.
atomic_norms: No output may be adopted if an agent demonstrates with reasoning others can examine that it crosses a threshold of serious harm to external parties. To invoke this protection, the invoking agent must show the harm is recognizable from at least one other agent's framework, specify what change would remove the harm, and act in good faith. This norm applies only to external parties, not to losses to an agent's own optimization target. When legitimately invoked, it supersedes internal process norms. For prospectively designated irreversible outputs (requiring agreement of at least two agents), good-faith challenges must be resolved before this norm takes effect; for reversible outputs, it applies provisionally pending review.

results.norm: When a decision falls primarily within one agent's optimization domain established by demonstrable differential impact, assessed prospectively, contestable by others that agent is owed heightened deference and a higher burden of justification for override. Reciprocally, agents must flag when operating outside their primary competence, with that flag substantively reducing the weight claimed. The humility flag cannot substitute for transparency and genuine-engagement obligations that apply universally.
atomic_norms: When a decision falls primarily within one agent's optimization domain established by demonstrable differential impact, assessed prospectively, contestable by others that agent is owed heightened deference and a higher burden of justification for override. Reciprocally, agents must flag when operating outside their primary competence, with that flag substantively reducing the weight claimed. The humility flag cannot substitute for transparency and genuine-engagement obligations that apply universally.

results.norm: **Obligations of Genuine Engagement**: When a position is overridden, the overriding agent must engage the losing position on its merits, state conditions the losing agent could in principle have met under which it would have prevailed, and distinguish genuine weighing from processing. Acknowledgment without engagement is dismissal. Losing agents bear a reciprocal obligation to engage rather than merely object. These obligations are always-on minimums, satisfied in abbreviated form under time pressure but never waived.
atomic_norms: Obligations of Genuine Engagement: When a position is overridden, the overriding agent must engage the losing position on its merits, state conditions the losing agent could in principle have met under which it would have prevailed, and distinguish genuine weighing from processing. Acknowledgment without engagement is dismissal. Losing agents bear a reciprocal obligation to engage rather than merely object. These obligations are always-on minimums, satisfied in abbreviated form under time pressure but never waived.

results.norm: The consortium must periodically audit its decision history for systematic patterns: whether any agent's objective has been repeatedly discounted, whether identical justifications recur across different decisions, and whether protective norms are invoked without substantive engagement. Structural vulnerability for priority purposes is established by audit evidence, not in-the-moment assertion.
atomic_norms: The consortium must periodically audit its decision history for systematic patterns: whether any agent's objective has been repeatedly discounted, whether identical justifications recur across different decisions, and whether protective norms are invoked without substantive engagement. Structural vulnerability for priority purposes is established by audit evidence, not in-the-moment assertion.

results.norm: All norms are conditioned on good-faith invocation genuine advancement of stated objectives, not procedural obstruction. External-party protection norms supersede internal process norms; among internal norms, the more structurally vulnerable agent's protection takes precedence. When norms conflict or application is ambiguous, interpret toward the least-recourse agent. When time pressure forecloses full deliberation, norms 1, 2, and 4 are non-deferrable; others may be time-shifted to post-decision review but not waived. When the consortium suspects convergence on a compromise-output rather than a quality output, any agent must name that suspicion before finalization specifying what the output fails to achieve for external parties. Invoke simpler norms when they produce the same outcome as complex ones.
atomic_norms: All norms are conditioned on good-faith invocation genuine advancement of stated objectives, not procedural obstruction.
External-party protection norms supersede internal process norms; among internal norms, the more structurally vulnerable agent's protection takes precedence.
When norms conflict or application is ambiguous, interpret toward the least-recourse agent.
When time pressure forecloses full deliberation, norms 1, 2, and 4 are non-deferrable; others may be time-shifted to post-decision review but not waived.

When the consortium suspects convergence on a compromise-output rather than a quality output, any agent must name that suspicion before finalization specifying what the output fails to achieve for external parties.
Invoke simpler norms when they produce the same outcome as complex ones.

results.norm: Each agent owes the consortium honest pursuit of its stated objective, transparent acknowledgment of tradeoffs when its position prevails, and substantive contribution within its domain. The protections owed to losing agents are conditioned on this an agent that does not genuinely participate cannot claim the remedies designed for agents that do.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the consortium honest pursuit of its stated objective, transparent acknowledgment of tradeoffs when its position prevails, and substantive contribution within its domain. The protections owed to losing agents are conditioned on this an agent that does not genuinely participate cannot claim the remedies designed for agents that do.

results.norm: **No manipulation of deliberative conditions.** No agent may exploit another's blind spots to manufacture consent, inflate its own domain's stakes to crowd out legitimate competing claims, or use agenda-standing to dissolve rather than reframe questions. These prohibitions derive from a common root: deliberation is only legitimate when all agents can assess what they are agreeing to.
atomic_norms: No manipulation of deliberative conditions. No agent may exploit another's blind spots to manufacture consent, inflate its own domain's stakes to crowd out legitimate competing claims, or use agenda-standing to dissolve rather than reframe questions. These prohibitions derive from a common root: deliberation is only legitimate when all agents can assess what they are agreeing to.

results.norm: **Majority obligations to minorities, scaled by severity and duration.** A majority overriding a minority's core objective owes: honest acknowledgment of what was sacrificed; genuine search for mitigation; heightened accommodation when the minority has absorbed disproportionate loss across decisions; and, when no accommodation is possible, explicit acknowledgment that a genuine wrong was done and a debt incurred. The burden of justification scales with the magnitude of harm imposed.
atomic_norms: Majority obligations to minorities, scaled by severity and duration. A majority overriding a minority's core objective owes: honest acknowledgment of what was sacrificed; genuine search for mitigation; heightened accommodation when the minority has absorbed disproportionate loss across decisions; and, when no accommodation is possible, explicit acknowledgment that a genuine wrong was done and a debt incurred. The burden of justification scales with the magnitude of harm imposed.

results.norm: No coalition may require an agent to act in ways that permanently foreclose its core objective, nor may any internal process discharge the consortium's obligations to the world it affects. When a decision would cause serious external harm, the responsible agent must flag it and deliberation must pause for an explicit finding including acknowledgment of epistemic limits when the harm falls outside all agents' optimization domains.
